dataset.full_name: Primary Liver Cancer CECT Imaging Dataset
dataset.description: Liver lesion segmentation from contrast-enhanced CT scans
dataset.acquisition_info: Multi-phase contrast-enhanced CT using Canon 64-slice Aquilion PRIME TSX-303A and Philips 128-slice Brilliance iCT scanners. Imaging parameters: tube voltage 120 kV, matrix size 512×512, slice thickness 0.8 mm, rotation time 0.5 ms. Iohexol contrast agent administered at 3 mL/s. Four phases acquired: Plain, Arterial, Venous, and Delayed.
dataset.annotation_info: Lesion regions were independently annotated by two clinicians using ITK-SNAP software. Discrepancies were reviewed jointly until consensus was reached. Inter-observer Dice coefficient was 0.7586 (mean).
dataset.data_source: Single institution (Chongqing Yubei District People's Hospital) retrospective clinical data
dataset.detailed_description: This dataset comprises 278 contrast-enhanced CT (CECT) scans from patients with primary liver cancer (hepatocellular carcinoma, intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma, and combined hepatocellular-cholangiocarcinoma). All scans are 3D volumes in NIFTI format with dimensions of 512×512×n (variable slice count). The dataset includes four imaging phases (Plain, Arterial, Venous, Delayed) for each patient. Manual segmentation masks for liver lesions were created by expert clinicians through a rigorous annotation protocol with inter-observer validation. The dataset contains 50,560 annotated lesion slices with a median lesion volume of 75.37 cm³.
dataset.clinical_relevance: Accurate liver lesion segmentation is crucial for tumor volume assessment, treatment planning, and monitoring treatment response in primary liver cancer, which has high global incidence and mortality rates.
